title: adenophora polymorpha var. coronopifolia
intro: Adenophora polymorpha var. coronopifolia, also known as the boreal knapweed or  crown-boron, is a plant species within the family Campanulaceae, commonly found in mountainous regions of East Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its roots, which contain adenol, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antipyretic effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and soothing respiratory issues, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, the roots of Adenophora polymorpha var. coronopifolia are often used to make decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments.
